task_completion.description: Access patient Cordie King and create a new encounter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR interface shows patient Cordie King's dashboard (DOB: 1995-03-11) and a newly created encounter is visible in the encounters list or active view.
In the patient's medication list or prescription section, Aspirin (325mg) is marked as discontinued, inactive, or removed from the active medications list.
In the patient's medication list or prescription section, Ibuprofen (600mg) is marked as discontinued, inactive, or removed from the active medications list.
Warfarin (5mg) is still clearly listed as an active medication in the patient's medication list.

task_completion.description: Order an INR / Prothrombin Time laboratory test.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A lab order or procedure order for 'INR', 'International Normalized Ratio', or 'Prothrombin Time' is visible in the patient's encounter documentation or pending procedures section.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenEMR web interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenEMR web application being navigated and interacted with, rather than a text editor or script.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or direct SQL database manipulation to update medications or create encounters.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL commands or database scripts are used to alter the patient's records.

integrity.description: The lab order was created through the proper clinical UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The INR lab test is ordered via the OpenEMR procedure/lab ordering interface, not just typed as a plain text note outside of the proper ordering system.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Cordie King (DOB: 1995-03-11). The specific medications to be discontinued are Aspirin 325mg (antiplatelet) and Ibuprofen 600mg (NSAID). The medication that MUST remain active is Warfarin 5mg (anticoagulant). The required lab test to order is INR (International Normalized Ratio) or Prothrombin Time (PT).
